title: runtime headless headless-verify
purpose: I3.1 defines runtime headless headless verify for the agent-driven expert system. Runtime & integration — IDE, SDK, headless, MCP tools.
scope: Owns `I3.1` only; siblings under `I3` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).
Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §11 — Branch I — Runtime & integration plane](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#11-branch-i-runtime-integration-plane).
behavior_steps: Scheduled CI or an operator invokes `scripts/headless-verify.py`, which reads `state.json`, task cards, and configured evidence paths from disk without spawning a conductor LLM turn unless explicitly configured.

The script runs [B1.1](B1.1-s0-deterministic-mandatory-first.md) verify-router and validate-workflow for the active pursuit step, executing the same Test commands task cards declare for IDE verifier workers.
Passing runs append immutable logs under `evidence/` per the [H4](H4-evidence.md) contract so headless output aggregates into goal_verify the same way IDE-captured evidence does.
On failure, headless-verify sets `last_verify` to failed in `state.json` and records log paths so the conductor can resume on the next IDE continue without assuming prior checks still hold.
If `state.json` is corrupt, required task cards are missing, or evidence paths are stale, headless mode fails closed and surfaces structured H2 rather than marking verification passed.
edge_cases: Operator closes laptop mid-loop â€” state.json must resume from last good dual-write.
Concurrent manual edit to queue JSON â€” conductor reloads queue each wake; last writer wins with journal note.
Edge case `I3.1` variant 3: verify state dual-write before continuing pursuit.
Edge case `I3.1` variant 4: verify state dual-write before continuing pursuit.
Pass 3: add regression test or evidence path specific to `I3.1`.
Pass 3: cross-link related nodes in same branch index.
failure_modes: **Silent stop:** Agent ends turn without updating queue â†’ mitigated by /loop + check-hierarchy-queue.py EMPTY gate.
**False complete:** Item marked done without artifact â†’ audit-hierarchy-depth.py re-enqueues deepen pass.
**Scope bleed:** Worker edits journal/state during planning-only expansion â†’ forbidden in vision-expansion-prompt.
**Stale design:** Upstream vision Â§ changes â†’ reconcile-stale adds deepen items for affected ids.

narrative: I3.1 defines runtime headless headless verify for the agent-driven expert system. Runtime & integration — IDE, SDK, headless, MCP tools. In practice, the system scheduled ci or an operator invokes `scripts/headless-verify.py`, which reads `state.json`, task cards, and configured evidence paths from disk without spawning a conductor llm turn unless explicitly configured, then the script runs [b1.1](b1.1-s0-deterministic-mandatory-first.md) verify-router and validate-workflow for the active pursuit step, executing the same test commands task cards declare for ide verifier workers.
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent plane-i 2026-06-28 -->

`scripts/headless-verify.py` runs verify-router and validate-workflow without an IDE session—used locally and as the pattern for CI verify steps. Headless mode reads state.json and task cards from disk; it does not spawn conductor LLM turns unless explicitly configured.

Output writes to evidence/ with the same immutability contract as IDE verifier ([H4](H4-evidence.md)). Failures set last_verify failed in state for conductor resume on next IDE session.
